sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 2-week history of right shoulder pain that has been persistent and has not resolved with rest.
He reports that the pain is worse with movement and is located on the outside of the shoulder.
He denies any numbness in the arm or hands.
He has had some similar pain in the past, but this episode is more severe.
He has been taking Advil and icing the area, which provides some relief.
He has a history of playing baseball and injuring the same shoulder, but this pain is not in the same location.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of playing baseball and injuring the right shoulder, but this is not mentioned as a current problem.
He is not taking any medications other than Advil for the shoulder pain.
He is not a smoker, and his occupation is not mentioned.
Review of systems: No other symptoms are mentioned.
Current medications:
Advil (dosage and frequency not specified) for shoulder pain
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are as follows: temperature 97.8°F, heart rate 62, respirations 16, blood pressure 116/84, and oxygen saturation 99%.
Physical exam: The patient has limited active and passive range of motion of the right shoulder, with tenderness at the greater tuberosity of the humerus.
There is no tenderness at the sternoclavicular or acromioclavicular joints.
The patient has a good hand grip, and the neurovascular exam of the right arm is intact.
Test Results: The patient's shoulder X-ray shows no fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy.
Problem list: Rotator cuff tendinopathy; Acute shoulder pain; [NOT MENTIONED]; [NOT MENTIONED]; [NOT MENTIONED]; Foot pain; Ankle pain; [NOT MENTIONED]; [NOT MENTIONED]; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed Tylenol for the shoulder pain.
I ordered an MRI to further evaluate the shoulder.
I referred the patient to physical therapy for 6-8 weeks.
I recommended that the patient continue to take Tylenol and consider a steroid injection if the symptoms do not improve.
I also suggested that the patient try soaking their feet in Epsom salts to alleviate foot pain.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in 2 weeks to review the MRI results and progress with physical therapy.
